falsificationAttempts.targetDescription: Disprove that sample-level score nulls are expected on modern subset runs.
falsificationQueries: Inspect score-stage apply paths and run orchestrator target selection.
Compare clean-run sample rows to unit-level score rows.
falsificationAttempts.falsificationAnalysis: Modern runs target and patch `sample_evidence_scores` for scoring. Clean D1 data shows full unit-level score completion with null sample-level score fields.

falsificationAttempts.targetDescription: Disprove that terminal artifact-derived accounting is mostly correct on representative partial runs.
falsificationQueries: Compare `getRunSummary` against artifact table counts on partial D1 and partial P1 runs.
Compare exhausted target counts to downstream blocked-stage misses.
falsificationAttempts.falsificationAnalysis: Terminal summaries largely match artifact truth, but telemetry semantics drift in some surfaces and event payloads. This weakens an over-broad version of the claim but not the core artifact-accounting conclusion.

falsificationAttempts.targetDescription: Disprove that parser/output fragility is the dominant remaining pre-launch risk in the reviewed failures.
falsificationQueries: Inspect failed request distributions and top error messages on the bad P1 run.
Check whether representative failed runs ended on exhausted requests rather than scheduler inactivity.
falsificationAttempts.falsificationAnalysis: Parse failures dominate the bad P1 run and appear in D1 too, but telemetry/OCC noise remains a secondary operational risk. The hypothesis survives in weakened form.
